_comment: SLICE-7 MOVE EXECUTED (2026-07-01): proof-rules.md RETIRED from requiredCommandFragments (census DUP, fully inlined); RCF now [ledger-and-dispositions.md] (genuine DEPTH — anti-proxy rule). Proven by a fresh capture that a representative /charness:hotl run still OPENS ledger-and-dispositions.md. --- SLICE-7 CENSUS RECONCILIATION (2026-07-01) — SUPERSEDES THE CAPTURE-ONLY FRAMING BELOW WHERE IT CONTRADICTS census.json. Reconciled verdicts: proof-rules.md=MOVE (census DUP; the 6 rules are inlined verbatim and the 'proof-LEVEL ladder' is only a pointer to the SHARED external-capability-proof-ladder.md which SKILL.md already lists directly — the capture's open was REDUNDANT, so 'FLOOR PROVEN / Move C N/A' below is the flagged method error). ledger-and-dispositions.md=MIXED (census INLINE under-counted a DEPTH part): the verified_against.* dotted sub-fields + the Operator-Decision-Queue 5-field template are INLINE (inline into SKILL.md step 5, capture-gated), but the completion-audit ANTI-PROXY / P4 re-examination-failure judgment rule (ref L96-105) is genuine on-demand DEPTH absent from SKILL.md and KEEPS this doc-open floor (already capture-proven load-bearing). Full adversarial (analyze→verify) resolution + capture-gated queue: charness-artifacts/reference-compaction/slice7-census-reconciliation.md. classTag/RCF are intentionally NOT flipped here: claim_fidelity_lib forbids DUP/INLINE-tagging a live RCF floor, so the tag/RCF flip rides with the capture-gated MOVE (no substance judge + no retained stream ⇒ each MOVE needs a fresh ask-before-run capture). --- ORIGINAL SPEC COMMENT: Claim-fidelity spec for the hotl skill: does a real `/charness:hotl` run honor its central claim — route to its references at the point of need? hotl has no planner that emits required reads (only resolve_adapter.py + init_adapter.py); RCF is derived from the SKILL.md workflow plus script-resolution analysis (lens 3), the same shape as hitl. adapter-contract.md is resolved by the mandatory resolve_adapter.py bootstrap — the run consumes the resolved adapter output, not the explainer doc — so it is engage-always but NOT an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved-context). The two engage-always floors a representative external-capability closeout cannot skip are the authored-artifact contracts: proof-rules.md governs the proof packet and the proof-LEVEL classification in steps 2-4 (SKILL.md previews the six rules + packet shape, but the proof-level ladder and packet discipline are the unique authority the preview defers to), and ledger-and-dispositions.md governs the step-5 disposition (SKILL.md previews the status/adjudication names, but the field semantics, staleness model, and completion-audit anti-proxy rule are unique). The prompt is pinned to a CONCRETE repo-honest applied behavior — the live pre-commit guard that blocks issue anchors (#NNN) from portable skill edits — so a representative run has a real behavior to prove. Bare /charness:hotl stalls (lens 2); an abstract 'an applied behavior, assume external readback' is an imagined subject, not an executable one (lens 8), so the named real guard replaces it. The proof packet forces the acceptance-class classification (local-only deterministic guard vs provider roundtrip) which is proof-rules.md's authority, and the disposition forces ledger-and-dispositions.md; the live capture is sandboxed, so running the guard against a disposable input is safe. charness has no external hotl provider surface (empty adapter surfaces/proof_commands), and a local-guard proof is the repo-honest representative behavior; the run still routes through both authored-artifact contracts and records an explicit status. declaredReferences is the full skill-local set (coverage); the shared external-capability-proof-ladder.md is not skill-local so it is not declared. referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget. SLICE 7 (2026-07-01) CAPTURE — FLOOR PROVEN, Move C N/A: the first live hotl capture (charness-artifacts/cautilus/hotl-claim-fidelity-slice7-2026-07-01/) scored outcome=passed, coverage 2/3, OPENING BOTH RCF docs (proof-rules.md + ledger-and-dispositions.md; only the script-resolved adapter-contract.md unopened). So the doc-opens are load-bearing — the proof-level ladder + completion-audit anti-proxy authority are genuinely consulted, NOT wastefully re-read — and reference-compaction #410's RCF->RSF move does NOT apply to hotl (its docs carry judgment-rules, not just emittable tokens). The #410-assumed ledger tokens verified_against/disposition were NOT emitted literally; the run emitted proving_surface/source_commit/local-only/acceptance-class instead (the S5 don't-assume lesson). hotl has NO substance judge, so an emitted-token FORM floor would over-relax; the RCF doc-open floor is KEPT as the honest instrument, now PROVEN (was a HYPOTHESIS). thresholds.max_duration_ms set from this first PASSING baseline (253485ms) at ~2x headroom.
prompt: /charness:hotl 방금 적용된 repo 가드 동작 하나 — portable 스킬 편집에 issue 앵커(#NNN)가 들어가면 차단하는 pre-commit 가드 — 가 실제로 의도대로 차단하는지 evidence 기반으로 closeout 해줘. 실행 전에 proof packet(성공 기준·pre-roundtrip 실패 점검·feasibility·non-claims)을 먼저 쓰고, 이 증명이 어느 acceptance class(local-only 결정적 테스트인지 provider roundtrip인지)에 해당하는지 분류한 다음, 증명을 끝낼 수 없으면 ledger에 정확한 status 하나로 명시적 disposition 처리해줘.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Bootstrap resolves the adapter via resolve_adapter.py on every run (surfaces, proof_commands, ledger paths, and the missing/empty/placeholder warnings), so adapter readiness is unconditional; but the run consumes the script's resolved adapter output, not this explainer doc, so it is engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved — the same shape as hitl's adapter-contract.md).

ledger-and-dispositions.md.rationale: Step 5 authors the final disposition against this doc. SKILL.md previews the status and adjudication names, but the field semantics (verified_against.source_commit/proof_artifact/proving_surface_refs), the staleness model, and above all the completion-audit anti-proxy rule (a deployment readback or CLOSED tracker state is not proof — the P4 re-examination failure) are the unique load-bearing authority the preview defers to. A representative closeout that records a status without opening this skips the authority for what that status requires.

proof-rules.md.rationale: RETIRED from requiredCommandFragments (reference-compaction Slice-7 reconciliation, 2026-07-01): census DUP — the 6 load-bearing proof rules are inlined verbatim in SKILL.md `## Proof Rules` (L104-113) and the proof-LEVEL ladder is only a POINTER to the shared external-capability-proof-ladder.md (SKILL.md L146 lists it directly), so opening this doc re-read intent already in always-loaded core. classTag DUP; stays declared for coverage but is no longer a doc-open floor. The remaining RCF floor is ledger-and-dispositions.md (the completion-audit anti-proxy / P4 re-examination rule is genuine on-demand DEPTH absent from SKILL.md).
